ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu, mina ati, mɔɔyɔ́ si awɔ ágyu atɔ. Na lɛkpanku-ma nteeboku buɛɛ buɛɛ kalɛ na bebuo lisu lɛmmɔ badubɔkana busie kenye. Na lɛnu banamu bammɔ lɛnɔ na mbasiaku-ɔ na Seir kalɔ lɛlɔ.”
translated: "I have eaten the meal, I say, the offering has been made. Then the chief took the valuable gifts, the precious ones, and went to the city of the bad people. Then those people came there, and the man's name, Seir, spoke."

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Nsu Noa ɔ́sɔ ɔ́ya na emeyi maatɛ bamba ásɔ ávi asa na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́tala Abloduma ɔmmɔ. Nsu ka Abloduma ɔmmɔ úsie ni, ɔ́sɔ útenwini útebo-nwu kudɔ lɛnɔ.
translated: The waters of Noah's flood came and covered the earth with great waters, and the Abloduma was filled. The waters of the Abloduma began to recede, and the earth appeared again.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Mmɔ na Lea ɔ́su ɔnini, “Kuli kɔdia-mi geen!” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Gad, na nwuna kalɔ ndi lilidia.
translated: He and Lea went together, saying, "Let's go to the river!" Then he took the fish to Gad, and the women sang songs.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Mmɔ na ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Báatɔlɔ mɛni. Na línu lɛnɔ ka baasu banini, ‘Bɛya na buteesie Dotan kalɔ kemmo.’ ” Ɔmmɔ ɔ́su ni, Yoseƒ ɔ́sia nwuna banwani kama na útánu-ma na Dotan kudɔ mmɔ.
translated: Yosef’s brothers went to kill him, but they said, “Let’s make peace.” When the brothers saw him, they sold him for twenty pieces of silver, saying, “He will go to Dothan.” So he went there, and Yosef met his brothers in the field near Dothan.

ref: One day, Joseph went to Potiphar's house to do his work, and none of the other servants were there.
text: Limeyi lɛdi ni, Yoseƒ úsie útátaabla nwuna kaduli kabla na ɔtɔ kemmo ɔni na kabla ɔblane bamba ɔdi uuubo mmɔ.
translated: In the days of Limeyi, Joseph went up with his brothers to the mountain, took stones, and divided them into two groups as a sign to the people.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Mmɔ ni, Lot baƒɔ bammɔ báni Lot béduoku ɔtɔ na bákalɛ.
translated: And he said, Lot said to them; Lot went to Sodom and they were saved.

text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Unwoti ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Muunú likyulikpi lɛni na bekyuli bani nkya Sodom ku Gomora baabla. Bana ekpidi ávi ntu.
translated: And it came to pass, Unwoti spoke to Abraham's servant, saying, "The people of Sodom and Gomorrah perished because they did not heed my voice."

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Katɔti kusɛ kusɛ ɔsiaku-ɔ geen. Bo na buteesie. Tasɔ tenye libiɔ lɛmmɔ kɔtu lɛni. Lɛ́ɛklibi ɔtɔ ku bebuo bammɔ kakyakɔ ƒui líse.”
translated: And the corpse said, "Katɔti, come here quickly! But you have betrayed. Take the money and run away. Escape from here before they catch you and kill you."

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Amu na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Bɛnu, ɔƒɛ ɔmmɔ ɔɔbi na útedu tɔ ka baƒonu balɛ alakpi akɔ babasala tɔ kesii kadi. Anwuɔsu bite-ma ntu na bɛsɔku-ma binwini biteesienku kadikɔ.”
translated: Benu said to the people, "If you do not listen to my words and follow the path I have shown you, then the enemy will come and destroy you completely. If my people do not follow them, they will be utterly destroyed."

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Udeedi ɔni náka bɔlɛ ni, úgyie. Na nwuna lɛlɔ ƒui linwini lígyie. Ɔmmɔ ɔsu básia-nwu lɛnyi banii Esau.
translated: Jacob indeed took the birthright. And he went to Beersheba to live there. After his father's death, Esau sought to kill Jacob.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, ɔ́sɔ ɔ́tɔlɔ mmɔ lɛnɔ úsie útéku libiɔ bamba. Na ɔdi ɔ́sɔ útakya-nwu kadɔ kadi. Na ɔ́sia-ni lɛnyi ɔnii Rehobot iye Kuƒi Kɔtɛ. Na údie-nwu kalɔ ɔnini, “Ɔƒɛni amu Unwoti Katɔti úte-bu keliekɔ, na bɔdubɔlɛ lili na kalɔ kamɛni kemmo.”
translated: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth. And the earth was formless and darkness was upon the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the waters. And he said, Let there be light, and there was Rehobot and Kufi Kote. And the voice of the Lord said, "I am the Lord who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give you this land as an inheritance."

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abram ɔ́tɔlɔ Egyipte úti ɔ́kɔ úsie kesii kani baalɔ Negev. Na útiku ulekudi ku ala ani ƒui na ubo. Na Lot lɛnɔ ɔ́siaku-nwu.
translated: Now there came to pass, Abram went down to Egypt because there was a famine in the land of Negev. And after the time following, he returned to the place where he had been before. And Lot, his nephew, was with him.

ref: Abraham was worried about Ishmael.
text: Litemi lɛmɛni lɛ́kpulu Abraham kayi, ɔsika lɛdanku ubidi.
translated: The people of the land of Abraham died, and the land became desolate.

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Na Seir ɔni ndi Horini ku nwuna bebi bananabi bani nélie kalɛkɛ kammɔ lɛlɔ na bádi lɛga ndi Lotan ku Sobal ku Sibeon ku Ana
translated: The descendants of Seir the Horite were Lotan, Sobal, Sibeon, and Ana, who had many possessions in the land of Edom.

ref: I'm going to send a flood that will destroy everything that breathes! Nothing will be left alive.
text: “Na ndubɔya na ɔnantu ɔna ɔƒɔ kalɔ lɛlɔ ƒui na ɔyila lɛla biala mbo ngba udu.
translated: The one who sees with their eye works with their hand, speaks with their mouth, and their heart and time are good.

translated: And the time of the Gentiles will be fulfilled until the child of God appears, and the people of God will enter the kingdom. And there will be joy among those of the kingdom. Amen, O Lord.

translated: When the people gathered at the sacred place, they performed rituals with their hearts. King Noa then arrived and also performed the rituals with his heart at that sacred place.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Mmomu teesie mina ɔti kaduli bekyuli akɔ na ami ɔlati kɔdua kemmo na atáwia uleku ete mina ubi ɔnanabi.’
translated: The child teases me; I journey to Kaduli Bekyuli, and my heart is not faint, for the road is long and if I die, I shall not regret it.

ref: So Judah said, “What will we gain if we kill our brother and hide his body?
text: Mmɔ ni, Yuda ɔ́su nwuna banwani ɔnini, “Bi na bɔdubo bɔkɛ si bɔ́du bula ɔnwani na búti ala búbi nwuna ubugya?
translated: Listen, Judas, why have you betrayed me? What did I do to you that you have sold me for thirty pieces of silver?

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Na uwi biala na kɔdigbo kɔ́lɛ na kayaa ni, ndubo bunyimo lɛla lɛni na ami Katɔti línyimi ku lɛla biala nli ngba.”
translated: And when he returned to the village with the elders and the children, he told Katɔti the message he received in the village's house.

ref: Afterwards, we'll go to Bethel. I will build an altar there for God, who answered my prayers when I was in trouble and who has always been at my side.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama na bibo na buteesie Betel kesii kani na ndubɔtu lɛkpabi lite Unwoti Katɔti. Anwu nénu-mi bɔkpali uwi ɔni na ala eekpóku-mi, na ɔ́gyumana-mi kesi kesii na lɛ́vi.”
translated: He called his name Betel, saying, "This is the house of the lamp of Unwoti Katɔti. My soul has been delivered, and my strength has been restored, and my right hand has triumphed."

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Anwuɔsu Abram ɔ́ta mmɔ ku nwuna ala ƒui na úsie kesii kani na Mamre egyii enengudi enye na Hebron kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na mmɔ na ɔ́tu lɛkpabi úte Katɔti.
translated: Abraham's wife Sarah died at the age of 127 in the city of Kirjath-arba (Hebron), which is in Mamre, and he buried her in the cave of Machpelah in Hebron. After this, Abraham breathed his last and died.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Ka batu bammɔ bénwini bébo ni, bábasu Yakob banini, “Búsie ƒula ɔnwani Esau kudɔ. Na anwu ɔlati ɔsia ulu ɔdubo ubasiaku-ɔ. Na ɔdanku nkyuli eti maane ubunku lɛlɔ.”
translated: When the time came for Jacob to call his sons, he said to them, "I saw Esau coming with four hundred men. Let us not anger him by fighting, but greet him with gifts as he passes by."

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ka bodobodo uƒine ɔmmɔ únu kalɛ na Yoseƒ údie ɔnwani kɔna kalɔ na kɔdanku ala abuni ni, anwu lɛnɔ ɔ́su Yoseƒ ɔnini, “Ami lɛnɔ lígye kɔna ka nti kɔgyigyɛ ɛtɛ.
translated: Then they named Joseph's son Manasseh, saying, "God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house."

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Amu na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Bɛnu, ɔƒɛ ɔmmɔ ɔɔbi na útedu tɔ ka baƒonu balɛ alakpi akɔ babasala tɔ kesii kadi. Anwuɔsu bite-ma ntu na bɛsɔku-ma binwini biteesienku kadikɔ.”
translated: Amu and the old hunter ɔnini said, "Bɛnu, the chief has hidden the child in the forest where hyenas and lions rest. The ancestral food and the elders' rituals have been completely consumed."

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Ka kadi kégye lɛdigye tutuutu ni, Abraham ɔ́ta na útile nwuna ebuo ala utine ala na ɔ́bala-nwu ala kaduɛ. Amu na útiku nwuna kabla bablane inyɔ ku ubidi Isak. Na ka ɔ́yɔ ekyweni pii na edu kalɛ na weti wakyula akpala ammɔ ni, ɔ́yu ulu úsie kesii kani na Katɔti ɔ́tina-nwu mmɔ.
translated: When God tested Abraham, he took his son Isaac, the wood for the burnt offering, and went to the land where he was to sacrifice his son. As he was about to slaughter him, he saw a ram caught in the thicket by its horns, which God had provided.

ref: men. So Jacob told his children to walk with their mothers.

translated: They went to the village and bought some fish and meat, then they cooked the food with vegetables and ate it with their hands.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ka ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, ɔ́kya Rahel kɔgbu na úse kalɔ uuwulo momoomo.
translated: When she went there, Rachel called her sibling and spoke about all the children.

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Uwi ɔni Isak úte Yakob kusɛ kusɛ ɔ́wɔlɔ na Yakob ɔ́vi ɔɔlɛ́ kani na Esau lɛnɔ ɔ́si lɛku kemmo úbeduo.
translated: And Isaac called Jacob and lovingly blessed him, and Jacob took the blessing from Esau because he did not take anything from Esau's blessing.
